thesis_anchors_verified: Per chapter-2 thesis: '(b) act with full effort without grasping the outcome' — 2.31 carries the 'act with full effort' half by naming the role-bound duty as the binding force.

Per chapter-2 thesis: 'separate what is invariant under change from what is current implementation' — 2.31 closes the bridge by naming what is bound (the role-context), distinct from the imperishable substrate that the previous block (2.11-2.30) named.
Per chapter-2 thesis: voice is 'didactic, corrective, slightly stern' — 2.31 voice is appropriately directive, opening the karma-yoga block.
Per chapter-2 thesis: 'Duty is not optional, even when it is hard. Do not flatten this into follow-your-passion.' — 2.31 explicitly preserves this in falsifiability and counter_example.
ordering_check: 2.31 correctly follows 2.30 (the bridge-coda). 2.30 said 'now act'; 2.31 says 'and the action you must take is your role-specific duty.' The ordering is exactly right for the karma-yoga block opening. The four follow-up verses (2.32-2.35) develop the duty argument in additional registers (unsought-arrival, mid-execution-quitting-failure, akīrti-as-signal, peer-network-update). 2.31 sits at the head of the block as the doctrinal opener.
verdict_reason: The verse supports the chapter thesis directly and sits in the correct progressive position. PASS on both 10.3 and 10.4.
